title: Understanding How JavaScript Was Originally Used

content.problemStatement:
JavaScript was originally designed to run **only inside web browsers**. Its main job was to make websites interactive—like responding to button clicks, validating forms, or updating content without refreshing the page.

In those early days, the only place you could run JavaScript code was **in the browser’s developer console**. Every major browser like **Chrome, Firefox, Edge, or Safari** has a built-in console where developers can write and test small snippets of JavaScript.

For example, you could open your browser’s developer tools, go to the “Console” tab, and type something like:

```javascript
console.log("Hello from the browser!");
```

This would immediately display the message in the console.

 📌 **Important:** Back then, there was no official way to run JavaScript outside the browser. It was purely a client-side language designed for enhancing user interfaces on web pages.

---

You can now follow along with a **video demo** to see exactly how to run JavaScript code in the browser console.
